title: celtis aspera
intro: Celtis aspera, also known as the netted-bark hackberry, is a species of deciduous tree native to parts of Asia and Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Celtis aspera include treating fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Celtis aspera typically involve using the bark, leaves, or roots to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
